maup_note: Corridor rates are a second, coarser view over the same already-published block-level segments — a MAUP-aware complement, not a replacement. Both granularities are published side by side; a corridor's count and exposure are sums of segments that individually already cleared the k-anonymity floor and the Getis-Ord significance test, so corridors never surface a block that was withheld or non-significant on its own. Aggregation choice (the boundary you draw) can itself shift a rate — the Modifiable Areal Unit Problem — which is exactly why the block-level file remains the primary published unit and this file is always secondary to it.
